title: "KPop Demon Hunters" Wins Grammy for Best Song for Visual Media - Variety
text:
KPop Demon Hunters Grammy Achievement

Grammy Win:
The song "Golden" from the Netflix film "KPop Demon Hunters" won the Grammy Award for best song written for visual media. Performed by EJAE, Audrey Nuna, and Rei Ami, the track was "written by EJAE, Mark Sonnenblick, IDO, 24 and Teddy." This marks a historic achievement: "It marks the first K-pop song to ever win a Grammy."

Song of the Year Status:
"Golden" advances to the main Grammy telecast, where it remains nominated for song of the year. According to the article, the track "is still up for song of the year, which will be presented on the nighttime telecast."

Additional Context:
The song faces strong competition, as other notable artists have earned nominations in broader categories. EJAE made her own mark as "the first Korean-American female songwriter to be nominated for song of the year." The film itself achieved massive streaming success, generating 207 million equivalent full watches in the U.S. during 2025.
